blurb:
This is Brazilian all-you-can-eat done right, and it's become our go-to for celebrating birthdays or when we have out-of-town guests. The atmosphere is relaxed and friendly, not fancy, which makes it good for families. It's in a shopping center on Powers Ferry, and you'll know you're in the right place when you smell the grilled meat.

The barbecue is the star here, with servers bringing different cuts to your table until you tell them to stop. The cheese bread is addictive, and if you're there on the right day, try the feijoada stew. They also do breakfast, which is a nice surprise if you want something different on weekend mornings.

Come hungry because you'll definitely get your money's worth. It's perfect for special occasions or when you want to try something different without breaking the bank.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat because there are twenty different food vendors all in one place. It's right by the Marietta Square, so you can walk around downtown after you eat or grab something before a concert or event. The atmosphere is casual and bustling, like a really good food court but with better options.

Everyone in our family finds something they like here, from pizza to Asian food to sandwiches. It's great for lunch meetings because you can each get what you want and sit together. The variety changes, so there's always something new to try.

Parking can be tricky during busy times downtown, but it's worth it for the convenience. Perfect for lunch dates, family dinners, or when you want to try a few different things without committing to one restaurant.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This is hands down the best pizza we've found in North Atlanta. The crust is thin and crispy like you'd get in Italy, not the thick stuff most places serve. It's become our regular Friday night spot when we don't feel like cooking.

They use ingredients imported from Italy mixed with local stuff, and you can taste the difference. The margherita is simple but perfect, and their salads are actually worth ordering too. Good for date night or bringing the kids.

It gets packed on weekends, so either go early or be prepared to wait. But trust me, it's worth it.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328

blurb:
This is our go-to when we're craving authentic Indian food that doesn't break the bank. It's in a strip mall near Perimeter Center, so don't expect fancy decor, but the food is the real deal. Great for families or when you want to order a bunch of different dishes to share.

The biryani is what they're known for and it's legit. The portions are huge and come family-style, so bring your appetite or plan on leftovers. We always end up ordering way more than we can eat because everything sounds good.

Just know they stay busy on weekends, so either go early or be prepared to wait a bit during peak dinner hours.

This is where we go when we want really good Middle Eastern food without spending a fortune. It's tucked into a strip mall on Franklin Gateway, but don't let that fool you. The food is legit and the prices are great for the amount of food you get.

The shawarma is what I order every time, and the falafel is crispy on the outside and fluffy inside like it should be. They're good about making things vegetarian-friendly if you need that. The portions are generous, so it's perfect for a casual lunch or dinner when you don't want to cook.

It's super laid back and good for families or just grabbing takeout on your way home. Open every day until 9pm, which is handy for weeknight dinners.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is the kind of place that saves your weekend when nothing else is open. It's a classic diner with that neon sign on Cobb Parkway, open 24/7, which has been a lifesaver more times than I can count. Good for late-night cravings, early breakfast before a flight, or when you want comfort food without the fuss.

The menu is huge and covers everything from Greek specialties to big American breakfasts. I usually go for the breakfast even if it's 2pm because their pancakes are solid and the portions are generous. My kids love it because they can get breakfast for dinner, and honestly, so do I.

Just know the parking lot can get crowded on weekends, and service can be a bit slow during peak hours. But when you need food at any hour of the day or night, this place delivers.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is officially on my list of top dinner places in Atlanta. The vibe is upscale but not stuffy, perfect for girls' night out or date night when you want to try something different. Persian food isn't everywhere around here, so this feels like a real find.

The hummus and chicken shish kabobs are what everyone raves about, and the rice with lentils and raisins is way better than it sounds. Their cocktails are really well made, and save room for the tiramisu because it's worth the extra calories.

They have that weird break between lunch and dinner service, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Make a reservation for weekend dinners because word is definitely out about this place.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328

blurb:
This is where we take our parents when they visit or celebrate anniversaries. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and if you get a window table at sunset, you'll understand why people drive from all over Atlanta to eat here. Perfect for special occasions when you want to impress someone.

The seafood is what they do best, and you can taste the difference in quality. The atmosphere is intimate without being stuffy, and the service actually knows what they're doing. It's pricey, but you get what you pay for.

Make a reservation, especially for weekend dinners. And if it's a special occasion, mention it when you book. They do a nice job with celebrations.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339

blurb:
This is hands down the best French bakery in the area, and it feels like a little piece of France right on South Marietta Parkway. The atmosphere is bright and welcoming, perfect for weekend brunch with friends or grabbing fresh bread for dinner parties. It's become our Saturday morning ritual.

The baguettes are crispy on the outside and soft inside, just like they should be. Their quiches are perfect for lunch, and the petits fours are beautiful if you need dessert for a special occasion. Everything tastes authentic, which makes sense because the owners are actually French.

Get there early on weekends because the popular items sell out. They close at 3:30 on Sundays, so don't wait too late if you want brunch. Perfect for special breakfast dates or when you want to impress guests with real French pastries.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've probably heard. The atmosphere is vibrant and fun, perfect for a night out with friends or a casual dinner when you want something that actually tastes fresh. They're open until midnight most nights, which is a lifesaver.

Start with the Esquite if you've never had it. Their cocktail pitchers are dangerous in the best way, especially the Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit ones. The service is surprisingly good for how busy they always are.

Expect a wait on weekends, but it moves pretty fast. And yes, it's worth the drive from wherever you are in North Atlanta.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
